track: Track 3: Helium-3 2^3S_1 HFS — operator-level five-component Roothaan autopsy (FIRST multi-electron HFS in catalogue)

contact_density_breakdown.structural_note: Multi-electron triplet contact density: |psi_total(0)|^2 = |phi_1s(0)|^2 + |phi_2s(0)|^2 for orthogonal 1s, 2s orbitals (cross-terms vanish by orthogonality). The effective spin-weighted density = total/2 is what goes into the BF formula (single-electron-calibrated) to give A in the I·J convention with J=1. The factor 1/2 is the structural spin projection: ⟨Σ_i δ³(r_i) s_{i,z}⟩_{M_S=1} = total/2.

he3_plus_1s_sanity.note: He-3+ (single-electron hydrogen-like ion at Z=2) sanity check: standard convention (g_He3 = mu/I, m_p in mass slot) matches experiment at <0.1%. This anchors the framework's He-3 g-factor and contact density at the single-electron level; the multi-electron 2^3S_1 extension builds on this.

operator_level_I_dot_J.structural_note: He-3 at (I=1/2, J=1) and D at (I=1, J=1/2) give identical I.J eigenvalue spectra by Wigner 3j I-J swap symmetry (both are F=1/2, F=3/2 multiplet). Splitting multiplicity = 3/2 in both cases.

1_bohr_fermi.convention_note: He-3 nuclear g-factor is NEGATIVE (g_He3 ≈ -4.255). The A_hf is therefore negative; the SPLITTING magnitude is |3A/2|. Reference value 6739.701 MHz is the magnitude.

3_reduced_mass_recoil.structural_note: Framework-native rest-mass projection (Paper 34 §III.14) at He-3 with nucleus mass m_He3 ≈ 3 m_p. The mass enhancement of recoil over hydrogen is ~1/3 (smaller recoil ppm because m_He3 > m_p). Cross-register V_eN production module should reproduce LO Bethe-Salpeter at ~3% for He-3 (vs 2.86% for H).

nlo_opt_in_with_He3_nucleon_mass.structural_note: NLO recoil-mixing factor m_e/(m_e + m_He3) ~ 1.82e-4 for He-3 (vs 5.45e-4 for H, 2.72e-4 for D). Sub-ppm correction to LO Zemach shift in the electronic regime.

h21_cross_validation.structural_note: Zemach magnitude scales linearly with r_Z (Eides LO). He-3/H ratio in operator output equals He-3/H ratio of radii to machine precision: framework-native scaling is exactly Eides leading order.

4_zemach_operator_level.operator_level_verdict: §III.18 magnetization-density operator at He-3 (Z=1 basis scaled by Z=2 for nucleus) reproduces Eides leading-order Zemach scalar to machine precision; profile (Gaussian vs exponential) independence preserved; NLO opt-in negligible in electronic regime.

5_phillips_kleinman_orthogonality.detectability_verdict: §III.20 Phillips-Kleinman / core-valence orthogonality IS structurally detectable at He-3 2^3S_1 HFS precision: spread across hydrogenic-vs-Slater-vs-full-screen contact-density prescriptions = 105468.3 ppm (well above the ~ppm experimental precision and well above the H/D HFS framework residuals). This makes He-3 2^3S_1 HFS the first precision-catalogue entry where the framework's multi-electron screening prescription matters at the residual level — the §V.D candidate convention exposure is hydrogenic-vs-Slater for the 2s effective charge.
5_phillips_kleinman_orthogonality.structural_reading: The 1s electron sees the full nuclear charge (Z=2); the 2s sees a screened charge ~ 1.0-1.15 depending on prescription. Hydrogenic (Z_eff=2 for both) over-estimates |phi_2s(0)|^2 by factor 8 vs full-screen. The Slater-screening prescription (Z_eff_2s=1.15) sits between the two; the framework's preferred Layer-2 input would be the §III.20 PK calculation with the actual He 2s screened radial wavefunction. This is the §III.20 operator-level test point.

chain_table.projection_chain: §III.1 Fock ∘ §III.7 spinor ∘ §III.8 Wigner 3j (I·J) ∘ §III.22 bipolar harmonic (multi-electron triplet spin coupling) ∘ §III.20 PK orthogonality (Slater screen)

chain_table.component: 4. + Zemach r_Z(He-3)=1.965 fm via §III.18 op-level (Z=2 scaling)
chain_table.projection_chain: §III.1 Fock ∘ §III.7 spinor ∘ §III.18 magnetization-density operator at I=1/2, lepton_mass=1, profile=gaussian
§III.20 Phillips-Kleinman / core-valence orthogonality for the 2s effective charge
chain_table.status: L2 (Layer-2 input via Slater screen or §III.20 PK ab initio)

attributions_approximate.PK_orthogonality_wall: §III.20 multi-electron screening of 2s by 1s; ab initio composed-architecture PK at finite n_max would replace Slater Z_eff with §III.20 operator-level PK potential
attributions_approximate.multi_electron_QED_wall: LS-8a renormalization gap + multi-electron Pachucki-style corrections; framework-native scope ends at one-loop on single-particle Dirac-S^3
attributions_approximate.He3_polarizability_wall: W3 inner-factor (He-3 nuclear structure smaller than deuteron polarizability because alpha-particle-like binding is stronger than n+p)

layer_2_attribution.verdict: Cumulative residual -12803.5 ppm dominated by §III.20 PK orthogonality (multi-electron screening of 2s) and multi-electron Pachucki-Drachman QED corrections. Unlike H/D HFS where the framework-native residual sits ~10-300 ppm with the dominant wall being LS-8a multi-loop QED, He-3 2^3S_1 HFS surfaces the §III.20 multi-electron screening as the dominant Layer-2 content — this is the FIRST precision-catalogue entry where the framework's contact-density extraction convention is the load-bearing systematic, not multi-loop QED.

framework_native: Bohr-Fermi I·J Hamiltonian at I=1/2, J=1 (Clebsch-Gordan F=1/2, 3/2)
Multi-electron contact density from (1s)(2s) triplet (|phi_1s(0)|^2 + |phi_2s(0)|^2, orthogonal-orbital case)
Reduced-mass / cross-register V_eN recoil at LO (m_p → m_He3)
§III.18 magnetization-density operator at I=1/2, He-3, leading-order Zemach (with Z=2 scaling for He nucleus)
Layer_2_inputs: Z_eff(2s) via Slater screening or §III.20 PK ab initio
external_walls_named: §III.20 multi-electron screening of 2s by 1s (dominant; the spread across hydrogenic/Slater/full-screen is the first place a multi-electron screening prescription is load-bearing for a precision catalogue entry)
Multi-electron QED Pachucki-Drachman corrections (LS-8a wall, multi-particle generalization)
He-3 nuclear polarizability (W3 inner-factor, smaller than deuteron because alpha-particle binding is stronger)
honest_limitations: Multi-electron contact density extracted from naive hydrogenic-Slater orbitals; the framework-native ab initio composed-architecture treatment via §III.20 PK at finite n_max is a named follow-on.
He-3 nuclear g-factor is NEGATIVE; this autopsy operates on the magnitude of the splitting (3/2 |A_hf|) following experimental convention.

The Hylleraas r12 module (geovac/hylleraas_r12.py) provides higher-accuracy multi-electron wavefunctions but the 2^3S_1 case was a NEGATIVE result (+209% at omega=5 per the existing precision-catalogue sprint); the named closure path is Hylleraas-Eckart double-alpha extension. Using the existing Hylleraas single-alpha for this autopsy would not be reliable.
Zemach Z=2 scaling assumes leading-order linearity in Z; sub-leading corrections (e.g. Friar 1979) are below the ppm level for He-3.
scope_boundary.framework_native_architecture_used: Hydrogenic-Slater multi-electron contact density (the most defensible cleanly-specified prescription at this sprint's scope; the §III.20 PK ab initio extraction is the named operator-level closure target). The §III.18 Zemach module is used at the operator level for the Zemach component; the Hylleraas r12 module is flagged but NOT used because its single-alpha basis is known non-variational for the 2^3S_1 state.
